content: Dementia treatment includes cognitive enhancers, non-pharmacologic interventions, and management of behavioral symptoms. Alzheimer's/Lewy body/Parkinson's disease dementia: cholinesterase inhibitors (donepezil, rivastigmine, galantamine) provide modest symptomatic benefit for cognition/function; rivastigmine patch reduces GI side effects. Memantine (NMDA antagonist) for moderate-severe AD, can combine with ChEI. Vascular dementia: focus on vascular risk reduction (BP control, statins, diabetes management, antiplatelets); ChEI may provide modest benefit. FTD: no effective cognitive enhancers; ChEI may worsen behavior. Non-pharmacologic: cognitive stimulation, structured routines, caregiver education/support, environmental modifications (safety, reduce overstimulation), advance care planning. Behavioral and psychological symptoms of dementia (BPSD): first-line non-drug approaches (identify triggers, modify environment, music therapy, validation therapy). Pharmacologic only for severe distress/danger: antidepressants (SSRIs for depression/anxiety), antipsychotics (atypical—quetiapine, risperidone) only for severe agitation/psychosis unresponsive to non-drug measures (black box warning: increased mortality in dementia patients; use lowest dose, shortest duration, informed consent). Avoid benzodiazepines (falls, confusion, paradoxical agitation).
